title: Election Night

jokes.text: History is calling, and it won't go to voicemail.
jokes.setup: Grandiose moment of election night being historic
jokes.punchline: Mundane voicemail reference deflates the gravity
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of typical political gravitas with modern technology reference. Clean structure and good economy.

jokes.text: Amy, if he wins, our lobbying stock is gonna droop like a chimp's tits.
jokes.setup: Serious discussion about political consequences
jokes.punchline: Bizarre anatomical comparison
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and vivid imagery that's both crude and oddly specific. Classic Veep profanity with creative flair.

jokes.text: Off air I'm telling the Nazis that she's hiding in the attic.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining his two-faced media strategy
jokes.punchline: Extreme historical metaphor for betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Dan's sociopathic pragmatism. Dark humor that's shocking but character-specific and well-earned.

jokes.text: So, suck it, Fox.
jokes.setup: Professional news anchor discussing their expert guest
jokes.punchline: Sudden crude dismissal of competitor
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment of professional mask slipping. Gets a chuckle but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey, wait, it's the testicle man. Guilty as charged. Check 'em, don't neglect 'em.
jokes.setup: Recognition of Jonah from his testicular cancer campaign
jokes.punchline: Jonah proudly owning the embarrassing nickname and repeating his slogan
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jonah's complete lack of self-awareness makes him embrace what should be humiliating.

jokes.text: You're the face of workplace bullying and genital health.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Jonah's public profile
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combination of serious issues
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist pairing of completely unrelated serious topics that somehow makes perfect sense for Jonah's career.

jokes.text: Oh, man, election nights are my cocaine. It used to be election nights and cocaine were my cocaine, but...
jokes.setup: Mike expressing enthusiasm for election night
jokes.punchline: Reveals past drug problem in casual aside
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure where the metaphor becomes literal mid-sentence. Perfect character reveal that's both funny and sad.

jokes.text: Then again, they said the Rapture was close.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Fox News calling the election close
jokes.punchline: Reference to Fox's other dubious predictions
jokes.explanation: Solid topical joke about media credibility. Well-structured but not particularly original or character-specific.

jokes.text: Okay, settle down. A bowl of hair could win those states.
jokes.setup: Team celebrating Vermont and Connecticut wins
jokes.punchline: Dismissing the victories as meaningless with absurd comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - deflating her own team's enthusiasm with a hilariously specific and vivid image. Economy and character integration are flawless.

jokes.text: What was that? I got, like, four seconds. Well, most people get just two.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about brief thank you time
jokes.punchline: Gary's matter-of-fact response that others get even less
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jonah's entitlement and Gary's dry response. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I'm saving that for when I go to prison. Even money they give you the chair.
jokes.setup: Bill not wanting to read projections
jokes.punchline: Ben's darkly comic escalation to death penalty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character-specific gallows humor. Ben's cynical nature comes through clearly.

jokes.text: I'll be thinking about that while I'm with whichever guy makes me his prison fiancé.
jokes.setup: Bill discussing having to ditch his real fiancé
jokes.punchline: Graphic prison rape implication delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate despair but relies somewhat on shock value. The casual delivery saves it from being purely cheap.

jokes.text: Fuck! / What? / Cramp.
jokes.setup: Selina suddenly exclaiming 'Fuck!' during tense election coverage
jokes.punchline: Gary's concerned reaction followed by the mundane explanation of a cramp
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that works because of the tense context and Gary's immediate concern.

jokes.text: That was transcendent bullshitting.
jokes.setup: Dan and Amy giving non-answers on TV
jokes.punchline: Dan admiring their own professional evasion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan moment - professional pride in being duplicitous. Self-aware and character-specific.

jokes.text: You're gonna win in a slide. You know, marginally.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to be encouraging about winning big
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining it with 'marginally'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary - trying to be supportive but accidentally deflating. Great structure with immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: Ah! These damn hotel carpets. I keep getting that static electric shock. You need one of those ionic bracelets that grounds you.
jokes.setup: Karen complaining about static shocks
jokes.punchline: Completely earnest new-age solution
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate new-age nonsense that shows Karen's personality. Minor but consistent.

jokes.text: It's the prison toilet situation which preys on one's mind. Defecating in full view of another man is unthinkable.
jokes.setup: Bill's prison anxieties continuing
jokes.punchline: Overly formal language for bathroom fears
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Bill's formal speech pattern making crude topic even funnier.

jokes.text: Yeah, Bill, put a cork in it, will you? You know, that actually may help.
jokes.setup: Ben telling Bill to stop talking about prison bathrooms
jokes.punchline: Bill taking the cork suggestion literally
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with Bill completely missing the metaphorical meaning. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: He's like a Native American tracker. He can hear it in the wind.
jokes.setup: Someone detecting an incoming call
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic comparison to mystical tracking abilities
jokes.explanation: Vivid and unexpected comparison that elevates a mundane moment. Good imagery but not character-specific.

jokes.text: I could pardon you? That wouldn't look good. You'd be the first person to say that. I'm not entirely sure I would in this circumstance.
jokes.setup: Bill asking about a potential pardon
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutal honesty about not helping him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina ruthlessness - completely self-interested even when a friend needs help. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Hey, Bill, I got a lot on the line with my presidency right now, so I don't really have time to be thinking about your whatever the fuck it is. Probable imprisonment. Exactly.
jokes.setup: Selina dismissing Bill's concerns
jokes.punchline: Bill helpfully clarifying what she's dismissing
jokes.explanation: Excellent character dynamics - Selina's dismissiveness and Bill's pathetic helpfulness. Strong structure and timing.

jokes.text: Set up an office with Selina at the Betty Ford Clinic?
jokes.setup: Asked what he'll do if O'Brien wins
jokes.punchline: Implies Selina will need addiction treatment
jokes.explanation: Sharp, dark prediction about Selina's future that feels earned given the characters and situation.

jokes.text: Politics bores me. It bores the living hell out of me. God, it's boring.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining his job application outside politics
jokes.punchline: Escalating repetition of how boring his career is
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dan's cynicism and self-awareness. The repetition works for emphasis.

jokes.text: I only really know Amy as the woman who rushed everywhere clutching her phone like it contained her frozen embryos.
jokes.setup: Karen describing her impression of Amy
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison to fertility preservation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and unexpected comparison that perfectly captures both Amy's phone obsession and adds a fertility anxiety layer.

jokes.text: Works frantically to avoid dealing with her weird mix of lack of self-worth and narcissism. I really like her.
jokes.setup: Karen analyzing Amy's psychological issues
jokes.punchline: Cheerful conclusion that she likes her
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Karen's blunt psychological assessment followed by sincere affection. Great contrast.

jokes.text: That was the bad news, which in the end I didn't bear.
jokes.setup: Kent saying he doesn't want to bear bad news
jokes.punchline: Immediately delivering it then noting the irony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent - overly literal and self-aware about his own contradictions. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: The bigness. Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: TV pundit talking about O'Brien's 'big win'
jokes.punchline: Dan repeating 'bigness' with mock seriousness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective mockery of TV punditry language. Dan's cynical nature comes through.

jokes.text: O'Brien can't be president. He's still contracted to be the KFC logo.
jokes.setup: Discussion of O'Brien's presidential chances
jokes.punchline: Bizarre claim about corporate endorsement resemblance
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and specific visual joke. Great economy and originality, though not character-specific.

jokes.text: About something specific, or...? Should the president and I have swapped roles? For? For the election. This election. There's an election going on.
jokes.setup: Selina asking Karen a hypothetical about swapping roles
jokes.punchline: Karen's complete confusion about basic current events
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Karen's complete disconnection from reality. Selina's escalating frustration is palpable.

jokes.text: Call me when you get this. Get someone else to dial if you're in a straightjacket.
jokes.setup: Dan leaving voicemail for Amy
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she might be institutionalized
jokes.explanation: Classic Dan cruelty disguised as concern. The practical suggestion makes it funnier and more character-specific.

jokes.text: Some of our viewers were complaining that you're shrill anyway, so... Oh, shrill? No, no, I'm never shrill.
jokes.setup: Greg insulting Amy's on-air performance
jokes.punchline: Amy's increasingly shrill denial of being shrill
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of the accusation being true. Amy's defensive response proves the point beautifully.

jokes.text: Hey, could someone check the parking lot? I think all the alarms are going off.
jokes.setup: Amy getting increasingly agitated and high-pitched
jokes.punchline: Someone suggesting her voice is setting off car alarms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the 'shrill' joke. The mundane explanation makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Screw this whole sloppy, backseat blowjob of a night anyway. I don't give a shit.
jokes.setup: Amy's mounting frustration
jokes.punchline: Bizarre sexual metaphor for disappointing evening
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and vivid metaphor that's both crude and oddly specific. Very quotable.

jokes.text: This is a 360-degree turn. It's 180.
jokes.setup: Someone misusing mathematical metaphor
jokes.punchline: Dan's correction
jokes.explanation: Standard correction joke. Gets the job done but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Rescinded is my new favorite word!
jokes.setup: Learning the Wisconsin call was rescinded
jokes.punchline: Childish enthusiasm for vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - finding joy in the most inappropriate things. Character-specific enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Look, look. Mom, CNN has it, too. It appears that Matty's domino is standing up straight again.
jokes.setup: Catherine excitedly showing her mom the news coverage
jokes.punchline: Anchor's unintentionally sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Decent double entendre that works on multiple levels. The innocent/sexual reading is well-constructed.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, you better unpack it. 'Let's see if we can unpack this now.' 'Oh, I've got pubic hair on my head.'
jokes.setup: Mocking TV pundit language about 'unpacking' news
jokes.punchline: Random insult about anchor's appearance
jokes.explanation: Somewhat cheap insult humor that doesn't feel particularly earned or character-specific.

jokes.text: Go back to the Shire, you fucking moonfaced hobbit.
jokes.setup: Anger at polling expert Matty
jokes.punchline: Lord of the Rings insult
jokes.explanation: Creative pop culture insult with good specificity. 'Moonfaced hobbit' is particularly vivid.

jokes.text: I mean, you know, everyone's like, 'Oh, Tom James. Ooh, it's Tom James. He's so dreamy. Sign my tits, Tom James.'
jokes.setup: Tom discussing his popularity
jokes.punchline: Crude fan request delivered in valley girl voice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's awareness of his sex appeal and slight discomfort with it.

jokes.text: I don't want to be impotent. In your administration. Oh, right!
jokes.setup: Tom's seemingly personal confession
jokes.punchline: Clarification that he means politically powerless
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect timing. The double meaning works both politically and sexually.

jokes.text: You want to run my economy? Bank of Tom James.
jokes.setup: Tom asking to be treasury secretary
jokes.punchline: Tom's grandiose branding of himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's ego and self-promotion instincts.

jokes.text: I used six rolls of Magic Tape to stick it back together.
jokes.setup: Tom claiming he held the administration together
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous office supply metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image of using tape to fix government. Specific and unexpected.

jokes.text: Want your face on money or something? Want that TV? Take it.
jokes.setup: Selina's sarcastic response to Tom's demands
jokes.punchline: Escalating to giving away random objects
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Selina's frustration and sarcasm. The randomness of the TV offer is great.

jokes.text: They supply 90% of the country's paprika. Yep, we lobbied for them, so my hands are dirty with paprika money.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining his potential new job
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'dirty money' with spice reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels. The specificity of paprika makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I've known sailors less likely to go either way than this. I... pardon my ribaldry.
jokes.setup: Bill commenting on the close election
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre with formal apology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - sophisticated language for crude joke, then immediately acknowledging it. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Fuck Iowa. I'd say nuke it, but I think someone already did.
jokes.setup: Anger at losing Iowa
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Iowa is already destroyed
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Iowa being forgettable/unimportant. Good structure but not particularly original.

jokes.text: I could crack a walnut in my ass.
jokes.setup: Selina expressing how tense she is
jokes.punchline: Crude anatomical metaphor for muscle tension
jokes.explanation: Memorably crude and specific image. Peak Selina vulgarity with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Just remember, check 'em, don't neglect 'em. Nice. You guys remember the A-Team, right? A-Team? 'I'm the A-Team.'
jokes.setup: Jonah doing his testicular cancer catchphrase on stage
jokes.punchline: Awkward transition to random TV show reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Jonah bombing on stage with random references. Character-specific awkwardness.

jokes.text: they were supposed to be on the run, but, you know, they were in a really distinctive van. Yeah, what was up... What was up with that?
jokes.setup: Jonah attempting observational comedy
jokes.punchline: Obvious plot hole observation delivered poorly
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jonah's terrible comedy instincts. The joke is supposed to be bad, which it succeeds at.

jokes.text: I know that my job can be hard on you and that was a bit of a bummer, huh? Yeah, just a little bit.
jokes.setup: Selina acknowledging impact on Catherine's relationship
jokes.punchline: Catherine's massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their relationship dynamic. Catherine's restraint is well-played.

jokes.text: Yeah, and he was, like, 60. He was 35.
jokes.setup: Selina dismissing Catherine's ex as old
jokes.punchline: Catherine's correction showing Selina's obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect mother-daughter dynamic showing Selina's complete disconnection from Catherine's life.

jokes.text: men are horrible. Okay? I mean, I have to just tell it to you like it is, honey. But all men are awful. Really. And the key is to just find a man who's the least horrible.
jokes.setup: Selina giving Catherine relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Cynical lowering of expectations presented as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - terrible maternal advice delivered with complete confidence. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Horrible.
jokes.setup: Catherine showing Selina a photo of her father
jokes.punchline: Selina's immediate assessment of her ex-husband as 'horrible'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and economy - one word that confirms her thesis with personal evidence.

jokes.text: God, it always seems longer with Catherine.
jokes.setup: Selina returning from 20 minutes with Catherine
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of finding her daughter tedious
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest moment that perfectly captures Selina's terrible parenting. Character integration is perfect.

jokes.text: Just something with real sugar. No sweeteners or I'll shit my pants.
jokes.setup: Sue's beverage request
jokes.punchline: Medical necessity presented matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue moment - delivering crude information with professional efficiency.

jokes.text: They're ghosts. You can't... you can't concede.
jokes.setup: Amy arguing against conceding based on projections
jokes.punchline: Desperate metaphysical argument about vote tallies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's desperation to find any argument. The 'ghosts' metaphor is creative.

jokes.text: This fucking job sucks anyway.
jokes.setup: Selina deciding to concede the presidency
jokes.punchline: Dismissing the most powerful job on Earth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - ultimate sour grapes moment delivered with complete petulance. Character integration is flawless.

jokes.text: Time to turn that noose back into a necktie, buddy.
jokes.setup: Dan telling someone about the Pennsylvania reversal
jokes.punchline: Dark metaphor for avoiding disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark metaphor that's both vivid and perfectly economical. One of the best lines in the episode.

jokes.text: I'm taking the White House. Please leave the hot tub on.
jokes.setup: Selina's triumphant call to O'Brien
jokes.punchline: Petty domestic concern amid grand political victory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - mixing grand political triumph with trivial personal comfort. Brilliant character work.

jokes.text: We need to give them a hit of political meth.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing the restless crowd
jokes.punchline: Drug metaphor for political excitement
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that captures the addictive nature of political rallies. Good Jonah moment.

jokes.text: Way ahead of you. Shit, I'm just getting how to tie a tie.
jokes.setup: Team trying to research what happens in an electoral tie
jokes.punchline: Gary's search results are useless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary incompetence moment. The literal interpretation of 'tie' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You do your best. You try to serve the people and then they just fuck you over. And you know why? Because they're ignorant and they're dumb as shit. And that, ladies and gentlemen, is democracy.
jokes.setup: Selina's frustrated rant about election results
jokes.punchline: Bitter conclusion about democratic process
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selina moment - her complete contempt for voters disguised as wisdom. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Is it a dance-off?
jokes.setup: Discussion of what happens if House tie
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous competition suggestion
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist suggestion that gets a quick laugh. Good economy but not character-specific.

jokes.text: So you mean that I might lose this election to my fucking vice president?
jokes.setup: Realization about constitutional process
jokes.punchline: Selina's outrage at the irony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina outrage at constitutional irony. The personal betrayal angle is character-specific.

jokes.text: At least it'd be somebody from our team, ma'am. I mean, that's good, right? I didn't mean to make her cry.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to find silver lining
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret as Selina breaks down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - trying to help and making things worse. Good reaction beat structure.

jokes.text: With all due respect, you need to get your shit together now, lady. Ma'am up. You're still the leader of the free world.
jokes.setup: Ben addressing crying Selina
jokes.punchline: 'Ma'am up' as variation of 'man up'
jokes.explanation: Great Ben moment - practical leadership with clever wordplay. 'Ma'am up' is a nice gender-specific twist.

jokes.text: With all due respect, you need to get your shit together now, lady. Ma'am up. You're still the leader of the free world.
jokes.setup: Ben addressing Selina's breakdown
jokes.punchline: The crude pep talk with 'Ma'am up' instead of 'man up'
jokes.explanation: Great Ben moment - mixing crude motivation with gender-appropriate wordplay and reminder of her position.

jokes.text: A tie is unprecedented. So is becoming the first lady president. So is that jackoff becoming president through the back door.
jokes.setup: Selina responding to 'unprecedented' criticism
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo in political context
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with nice double entendre. Selina's competitive fury comes through clearly.

jokes.text: The rule book's been torn up now and America is wiping its nasty ass with it.
jokes.setup: Selina's rant about unprecedented situation
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor for constitutional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crude and vivid imagery. Perfect Selina vulgarity applied to constitutional crisis.

jokes.text: That fucking guy with his fucking charm and his fucking son and his fucking wheelchair with his spine all fucked up.
jokes.setup: Selina ranting about Tom's advantages
jokes.punchline: Culminating in cursing his disability
jokes.explanation: Peak Selina awfulness - cursing someone's disability. Character-specific but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: Amy Brookheimer who so successfully ran my campaign until she became unwell.
jokes.setup: Selina introducing Amy to the rally crowd
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive dismissal disguised as praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina passive-aggression. The public humiliation disguised as gratitude is masterful.

jokes.text: Ma'am, I seriously overstepped the mark going out onstage. That was a fuck-up. Yeah, I'll say. You might want to watch that if you become president.
jokes.setup: Tom apologizing for going on stage
jokes.punchline: Selina's threat about his potential presidency
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the power dynamics and Selina's vindictiveness even in 'victory.'

jokes.text: Ma'am, I seriously overstepped the mark going out onstage. That was a fuck-up. / Yeah, I'll say.
jokes.setup: Tom apologizing for going onstage
jokes.punchline: Selina's blunt agreement that it was indeed a fuck-up
jokes.explanation: Good Selina bluntness - no forgiveness or diplomatic response to Tom's apology.

tmdb_overview: On election night, Selina and her staff find their nerves growing frayed as each state result is called.
insight: Season finale lands 73 jokes in 33 minutes but falls short of elite—momentum stalls in final act.
